summary.headline: Netflix VOID introduces physics-aware video object removal with model release; NousResearch hermes-agent explodes to 1,251 stars/day; Gemma-4 ecosystem dominates with 5 models in trending top 20
summary.body:
April 6th's landscape is marked by the convergence of video world models, the continued Gemma-4 ecosystem takeover, and the explosive growth of AI agent tooling on GitHub. **Netflix's VOID** paper and model release demonstrate physics-aware video object removal that corrects downstream interactions — not just inpainting backgrounds — representing a genuine step forward in video editing intelligence. The model's 395 trending score with zero downloads suggests gated access generating strong anticipatory engagement.

The **papers roster refreshes** with several strong new entries: **Agentic-MME** introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating agentic capabilities (tool use + web search) in multimodal LLMs, while **AgentHazard** systematically benchmarks harmful behaviors in computer-use agents — together signaling that the agent safety evaluation infrastructure is maturing rapidly. **InCoder-32B-Thinking** tackles the underserved industrial code domain (chip design, GPU optimization, embedded systems), and **CoME-VL** demonstrates that combining contrastive and self-supervised vision encoders yields richer multimodal representations.

On the model side, **Google's Gemma-4 family now occupies 5 of the top 20 trending spots** including NVIDIA's NVFP4 quantization and Unsloth's GGUF — a level of ecosystem saturation rarely seen this quickly. **LiquidAI's LFM2.5-350M** enters as a notable edge-focused model, while **k2-fsa/OmniVoice** brings zero-shot multilingual voice cloning to the open-weight space. GitHub trends show **NousResearch/hermes-agent** as the biggest new entrant at 1,251 stars/day (26.4k total), and **openscreen** continues its meteoric rise to 2,749 stars/day.

researcher_notes:
**Netflix VOID is the most technically interesting model release today.** Video object removal that understands downstream physics interactions — collisions, bouncing, support relationships — goes beyond cosmetic inpainting into genuine scene understanding. The CogVideoX-based architecture combined with 395 likes but 0 downloads suggests Netflix is building community anticipation for a staged release. The accompanying paper (5 comments, highest engagement among new papers) confirms real technical substance behind the hype.

**The agent safety benchmarking infrastructure is quietly reaching critical mass.** Agentic-MME and AgentHazard, appearing on the same day, represent complementary evaluation frameworks: Agentic-MME tests whether agents use tools correctly and efficiently, while AgentHazard tests whether they can be induced to cause harm. Combined with yesterday's CORAL framework for autonomous multi-agent evolution, we're seeing the research community simultaneously building more capable agent systems and the safety infrastructure to evaluate them. This parallel development is a healthy signal.

**Gemma-4's ecosystem dominance is now unprecedented for an open-weight family.** Five variants in the trending top 20 (31B-it, 26B-A4B-it, E4B-it, E2B-it, plus NVIDIA's NVFP4 quantization) span the full compute spectrum from 2B-equivalent to 31B dense. The Unsloth GGUF quantization at 487k downloads and the dealignai abliterated variant emerging simultaneously show that the community is both deploying and customizing Gemma-4 at unprecedented speed. This level of ecosystem velocity — from lab release to production quantization to uncensored variants — compressed into weeks rather than months signals a maturing open-weight deployment pipeline.

**The edge AI race is intensifying with genuinely differentiated approaches.** LiquidAI's LFM2.5-350M uses a non-transformer architecture (liquid state machines) for edge deployment, Prism ML's Bonsai-8B pushes 1-bit quantization, Google's Gemma-4-E2B offers a 2B-equivalent any-to-any model, and k2-fsa/OmniVoice covers 650+ languages for zero-shot TTS. The common thread: each targets a different modality or hardware constraint rather than competing head-to-head on text benchmarks. Edge AI is stratifying into specialized niches.

**GitHub's AI agent tooling landscape is fragmenting productively.** NousResearch's hermes-agent (1,251 stars/day) joins Block's Goose (882/day), badlogic's pi-mono (355/day), and Microsoft's agent-framework (278/day, up from 72 yesterday) as distinct approaches to the agent-building problem. hermes-agent's tagline 'the agent that grows with you' and its NousResearch pedigree suggest a model-native approach, while pi-mono's all-in-one toolkit (CLI + unified LLM API + TUI + Slack bot + vLLM pods) targets the full deployment lifecycle. The fact that Google's on-device ML gallery (389/day, 17k total) is also trending confirms that on-device inference is becoming a mainstream developer concern, not an edge case.

papers.title: VOID: Video Object and Interaction Deletion
papers.summary: Introduces physics-aware video object removal that goes beyond inpainting backgrounds to correct downstream physical interactions (collisions, support relationships) caused by the removed object. Accompanied by a model release on HuggingFace.
key_findings: Current video object removal methods fail when the removed object has significant physical interactions
VOID corrects downstream interaction artifacts like collisions and support changes, not just appearance
Model released alongside paper, generating 395 likes with anticipatory community engagement

papers.title: Agentic-MME: What Agentic Capability Really Brings to Multimodal Intelligence?
papers.summary: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating agentic capabilities in multimodal LLMs, testing both Visual Expansion (tool invocation) and Knowledge Expansion (web search) with verification of whether tools were actually invoked, applied correctly, and used efficiently.
key_findings: Existing multimodal evaluations cannot verify if tools were actually invoked or applied correctly
Agentic capabilities (tool use + search) provide measurable intelligence gains beyond passive observation
The benchmark reveals significant gaps between tool availability and effective tool utilization

papers.title: AgentHazard: A Benchmark for Evaluating Harmful Behavior in Computer-Use Agents
papers.summary: Systematic benchmark for evaluating harmful behaviors in computer-use agents that maintain persistent state and translate outputs into concrete actions, creating distinct safety challenges beyond chat-based LLM safety.
key_findings: Computer-use agents create fundamentally different safety challenges than chat-based systems due to persistent state
Agents translate intermediate outputs into real-world actions, making harmful behavior directly consequential
Existing LLM safety evaluations are insufficient for agentic systems with tool and environment access

papers.title: CoME-VL: Scaling Complementary Multi-Encoder Vision-Language Learning
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of AI
papers.summary: Demonstrates that combining contrastive (CLIP-style) and self-supervised vision encoders yields richer dense visual features for VLMs than either alone, challenging the single-encoder paradigm that dominates current vision-language models.
key_findings: Self-supervised encoders capture richer dense features than contrastive encoders alone
Complementary multi-encoder architectures outperform single-encoder VLMs across tasks
The approach scales effectively, suggesting current single-encoder VLMs leave representational capacity on the table

papers.title: InCoder-32B-Thinking: Industrial Code World Model for Thinking
papers.summary: Targets the underserved industrial code domain (chip design, GPU optimization, embedded systems) where expert reasoning traces are scarce, using Error-driven Chain-of-Thought training to build reasoning capabilities for hardware-constrained code.
key_findings: Industrial software domains lack expert reasoning traces for hardware constraint reasoning
Error-driven Chain-of-Thought training bootstraps reasoning from limited industrial code data
32B-parameter model achieves competitive performance on chip design and GPU optimization tasks

papers.title: LOME: Learning Human-Object Manipulation with Action-Conditioned Egocentric World Model
papers.summary: An egocentric world model approach to human-object manipulation that generalizes across diverse object morphologies without per-object physics setup, replacing traditional physics-based animation with learned action-conditioned generation.
key_findings: Egocentric world models bypass the need for per-object physics modeling and manual setup
Action-conditioned generation enables manipulation across diverse object morphologies
The approach outperforms physics-based methods on generalization to novel objects

papers.title: MultiGen: Level-Design for Editable Multiplayer Worlds in Diffusion Game Engines
papers.summary: Addresses two fundamental limitations of video world models for interactive simulation: user control over environment for reproducible experiences, and shared inference where multiple players hold influence over the world state simultaneously.
key_findings: Current video world models lack user control for reproducible, editable experiences
Shared inference enabling multiplayer interaction is a novel capability for diffusion-based world models
Level-design tools allow user-controlled environment creation within diffusion game engines

papers.title: DynaVid: Learning to Generate Highly Dynamic Videos using Synthetic Motion Data
papers.summary: Addresses the inability of current video diffusion models to generate realistic highly dynamic motion by introducing synthetic motion data as a training augmentation strategy, targeting the scarcity of dynamic examples in standard datasets.
key_findings: Video diffusion models struggle with highly dynamic motions due to training data scarcity
Synthetic motion data effectively augments training for dynamic video generation
The approach enables fine-grained motion controllability that current models lack

papers.title: Xpertbench: Expert Level Tasks with Rubrics-Based Evaluation
papers.summary: Tackles the growing saturation of conventional LLM benchmarks by introducing rubrics-based evaluation for complex, open-ended expert-level tasks across diverse domains, moving beyond narrow answer-matching evaluation.
key_findings: Conventional benchmarks are plateauing as LLMs reach ceiling performance
Rubrics-based evaluation better captures nuanced expert-level reasoning
The benchmark reveals significant performance gaps on open-ended tasks that closed-form evaluations miss

papers.title: Omni123: Exploring 3D Native Foundation Models with Limited 3D Data by Unifying Text to 2D and 3D Generation
papers.summary: Addresses the scarcity of 3D training data by unifying text-to-2D and text-to-3D generation within a single foundation model, leveraging abundant 2D imagery to bootstrap 3D synthesis capabilities.
key_findings: Unified 2D+3D generation within a single model bridges the 3D data scarcity gap
Abundant 2D imagery can effectively bootstrap 3D synthesis capabilities
The approach demonstrates that native 3D capability can emerge from multimodal pre-training

papers.title: Brainstacks: Cross-Domain Cognitive Capabilities via Frozen MoE-LoRA Stacks for Continual LLM Learning
papers.summary: A modular architecture for continual multi-domain LLM fine-tuning that packages domain expertise as frozen adapter stacks composing additively on a shared frozen base, enabling domain acquisition without catastrophic forgetting.
key_findings: Frozen MoE-LoRA stacks enable domain knowledge addition without catastrophic forgetting
Additive composition on a frozen base allows modular domain expertise management
The approach enables cross-domain capability without full model retraining

papers.title: ActionParty: Multi-Subject Action Binding in Generative Video Games
papers.summary: Tackles the fundamental challenge of controlling multiple agents simultaneously in video diffusion world models, moving beyond single-agent settings to enable multi-subject action binding for interactive environments.
key_findings: Current video world models are largely restricted to single-agent control
Multi-subject action binding enables simultaneous control of multiple agents in generated scenes
The approach opens the path toward multiplayer interactive environments in diffusion-based world models

models.name: Gemma-4-31B-it
models.description: Google's flagship 31B dense instruction-tuned multimodal model now leads the trending charts at 995 score with 490k downloads. The dominant open-weight multimodal model of the moment.

models.name: Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled
models.description: Community-distilled 27B model transferring Claude 4.6 Opus reasoning into Qwen3.5 architecture. Now at 539k downloads and 2,349 likes — the most successful open-weight reasoning distillation, continuing to grow.

models.name: Qwen3.5-9B-Uncensored-HauhauCS-Aggressive
models.description: Uncensored 9B Qwen3.5 variant now at 725k downloads and 995 likes, maintaining its position as the most-downloaded trending model. Sustained demand for unrestricted open-weight models.

models.name: Qianfan-OCR
models.description: Baidu's InternVL-based OCR model surpasses 1,000 likes (1,006) as document intelligence demand continues rising. Growing downloads at 37.7k.

models.name: Bonsai-8B-gguf
models.description: 1-bit quantized 8B model for extreme edge deployment. At 430 trending score and 38.6k downloads, 1-bit inference is gaining real traction.

models.name: void-model
models.description: Netflix's physics-aware video inpainting model for object removal. 395 likes with 0 downloads suggests gated or staged release. Accompanies the VOID paper demonstrating interaction-aware video editing.

models.name: Gemma-4-E4B-it
models.description: Google's 4B-equivalent any-to-any model supporting both image-text-to-text and broader modality generation. 197k downloads at 355 trending — the any-to-any capability is a differentiator.

models.name: Cohere Transcribe 03-2026
models.description: Cohere's multilingual ASR model continues climbing with 805 likes and 121k downloads, solidifying its position as the leading open-weight transcription model.

models.name: OmniVoice
models.description: Zero-shot multilingual voice cloning and text-to-speech covering 650+ languages. 245 trending score and 39.9k downloads — a remarkable scope of language coverage for open-weight TTS.

models.name: LFM2.5-350M
models.description: LiquidAI's 350M-parameter non-transformer edge model using liquid foundation architecture. 229 trending and 14.9k downloads signal growing interest in alternative architectures for edge deployment.

models.name: Gemma-4-31B-IT-NVFP4
models.description: NVIDIA's FP4 quantization of Gemma-4-31B using Model Optimizer. 85.9k downloads in days demonstrates the speed of enterprise quantization deployment for the Gemma-4 ecosystem.

trending_repos.name: siddharthvaddem/openscreen
trending_repos.description: Free, open-source screen recording studio. Explosive growth continues at 2,749 stars/day (22.6k total, up from 20k yesterday), establishing itself as the dominant open-source Screen Studio alternative.

trending_repos.name: NousResearch/hermes-agent
trending_repos.description: NousResearch's AI agent that 'grows with you'. Biggest new entrant at 1,251 stars/day and 26.4k total — NousResearch's model-native pedigree brings credibility to the agent framework space.

trending_repos.name: onyx-dot-app/onyx
trending_repos.description: Open-source AI chat platform with advanced features supporting every LLM. Maintains momentum at 998 stars/day (25k total), confirming self-hosted AI infrastructure as a durable category.

trending_repos.name: block/goose
trending_repos.description: Block's open-source extensible AI agent with install, execute, edit, and test capabilities across any LLM. Sustained at 882 stars/day (37k total), now the most-starred dedicated AI agent repo.

trending_repos.name: Blaizzy/mlx-vlm
trending_repos.description: Vision Language Model inference and fine-tuning on Apple Silicon via MLX. Accelerating to 416 stars/day (3.9k total, up from 343 yesterday), riding the Gemma-4 multimodal wave.

trending_repos.name: google-ai-edge/gallery
trending_repos.description: Google's gallery showcasing on-device ML/GenAI use cases with local model execution. New entrant at 389 stars/day (17k total) — Google pushing on-device inference as a first-class developer experience.

trending_repos.name: badlogic/pi-mono
trending_repos.description: All-in-one AI agent toolkit: coding agent CLI, unified LLM API, TUI & web UI, Slack bot, vLLM pods. 355 stars/day at 31.9k total — the full-lifecycle approach to agent deployment.

trending_repos.name: microsoft/agent-framework
trending_repos.description: Microsoft's framework for building and deploying AI agents and multi-agent workflows. Surging to 278 stars/day (8.9k total, up from 72/day yesterday) — Microsoft accelerating its agent platform play.

trending_repos.name: HKUDS/RAG-Anything
trending_repos.description: All-in-one RAG framework from HKUDS. At 99 stars/day and 15.2k total, the RAG tooling ecosystem continues to consolidate around comprehensive frameworks.

trending_repos.name: google-ai-edge/LiteRT-LM
trending_repos.description: Google's lightweight runtime for on-device LLM inference. 124 stars/day at 1.6k total — paired with the Gallery app, shows Google building a complete on-device AI stack.

trending_repos.name: dmtrKovalenko/fff.nvim
trending_repos.description: Ultra-fast file search toolkit for AI agents, Neovim, Rust, C, and NodeJS. 76 stars/day at 3.7k total — AI-optimized file search is becoming an infrastructure primitive.

themes.name: Physics-Aware Video Intelligence
themes.description: Netflix's VOID, DynaVid, MultiGen, and ActionParty collectively show video diffusion models moving from visual quality to physical understanding — correcting interaction artifacts, generating dynamic motion, and enabling multi-agent control.

themes.name: Agent Safety and Evaluation Infrastructure
themes.description: Agentic-MME and AgentHazard appearing on the same day represent complementary evaluation frameworks — capability benchmarking and safety benchmarking — for the agent paradigm. The research community is building evaluation infrastructure in parallel with capability.

themes.name: Gemma-4 Ecosystem Saturation
themes.description: Five Gemma-4 variants in the trending top 20 (dense, MoE, any-to-any, GGUF, NVFP4) spanning 2B to 31B parameters represents unprecedented ecosystem velocity for an open-weight family. The community deployment pipeline from release to production has compressed to weeks.

themes.name: AI Agent Framework Competition
themes.description: NousResearch hermes-agent (1,251 stars/day), Block Goose (882/day), pi-mono (355/day), and Microsoft agent-framework (278/day, 4x surge) represent four distinct approaches to agent building — model-native, extensible, full-lifecycle, and enterprise — fragmenting productively.

themes.name: Edge AI Architecture Diversification
themes.description: LiquidAI's non-transformer LFM2.5-350M, Prism ML's 1-bit Bonsai-8B, Google's any-to-any Gemma-4-E2B, and k2-fsa's 650-language OmniVoice each target different edge niches, signaling that edge AI is stratifying by modality and hardware constraint rather than converging.

themes.name: On-Device ML as Developer Platform
themes.description: Google AI Edge Gallery (389 stars/day), LiteRT-LM (124/day), and Blaizzy mlx-vlm (416/day) show on-device inference becoming a first-class developer platform concern, not just a research curiosity.
